cards.title: Art And Literature Prizes
cards.question: Name any winner of the Booker Prize, or any winner of the Turner Prize.

cards.title: German Experimental Rock And Electronic Music
cards.question: Name any German experimental rock ('krautrock') or electronic act, likely from a published list (Kraftwerk would score high).

cards.title: Secretaries Of State For Scotland And Wales
cards.question: Full name of anyone who has served as Secretary of State for Scotland or for Wales (the Wales post dates from 1964).

picks.note: Scotland under Callaghan, then a European Commissioner
two stints at the Wales Office in the 2000s

cards.title: The Royal Ascot Gold Cup
cards.question: Name any horse that has won the Gold Cup at Royal Ascot, probably since 1950 (Yeats, Stradivarius and Estimate would score high).

cards.title: TV Series Starring Jason Bateman
cards.question: Name any TV series in which Jason Bateman had a regular acting role (Ozark and Arrested Development would score high).

picks.note: a.k.a. Valerie - five seasons, zero UK recall

cards.title: World's Strongest Man & Woman
cards.question: Name any winner of World's Strongest Man (since 1977) or of World's Strongest Woman (Geoff Capes and Eddie Hall would score high).

picks.note: Finnish two-time winner of the late 90s
British 1989 winner lost in Capes's shadow

cards.title: Andy Murray
cards.question: Name any tournament at which Andy Murray won an ATP Tour singles title (2006-2019).

cards.title: Female Rappers
cards.question: Name any female rapper, most likely one with a UK top 40 single (Nicki Minaj and Cardi B would score high).

picks.note: British, six UK top 40 hits around 1990
London duo with late-80s chart singles
'Wee Rule' reached the UK top 10 in 1988
US pioneer with UK chart entries
battle-rap pioneer, near-zero with a UK panel

cards.title: British Crime Fiction Authors With The Surname 'James'
cards.question: Name any novel by P. D. James, or any novel by Peter James.

cards.title: Gilbert And Sullivan
cards.question: Name any of the fourteen Gilbert and Sullivan comic operas (The Mikado and The Pirates of Penzance would score high).

picks.note: pre-Pinafore and overshadowed by everything after

cards.title: The Men's Cricket World Cup 2023
cards.question: Name any member of the India or Australia squads from the 2023 final, or any of the ten competing nations (Kohli and Cummins would score high).

picks.note: India squad member who barely played
in the Australian squad, no final appearance
if nations count - the team everyone forgets qualified

cards.title: 'Grace' & 'Grace' On TV
cards.question: Most likely anyone with an acting credit in two TV 'Graces' - plausibly ITV's Grace and Grace and Frankie (pairing inferred from the card's quote marks).

picks.note: DS Branson in ITV's Grace; John Simm would score high

cards.title: P G Wodehouse On TV
cards.question: Name anyone with an acting credit in a TV adaptation of P. G. Wodehouse - Jeeves and Wooster (1990-93), Blandings (2013-14) or Wodehouse Playhouse (Fry and Laurie would score high).

picks.note: Tuppy Glossop in Jeeves and Wooster

cards.title: 'Boys' On TV
cards.question: Most likely anyone with an acting credit in two 'Boys' series - plausibly Amazon's The Boys or Mrs Brown's Boys (pairing inferred from the card's quote marks).

picks.note: Frenchie in The Boys; faces known, names not
Ashley in The Boys, deep-cast territory
Mother's Milk - third lead, rarely named
Dermot in Mrs Brown's Boys, anonymous off-screen

cards.title: Male England Footballers Overseas In 2023/24
cards.question: Name any male England international who played for a club outside England in the 2023/24 season (Bellingham and Kane would score high).

picks.note: AC Milan centre-back, capped but uncelebrated
at Roma, injured most of the season - easily forgotten
January loan to Dortmund - the move people half-remember

cards.title: Northern And Southern Hemisphere Wine Regions
cards.question: Name any recognised wine region in either hemisphere, likely from a defined list (Bordeaux, Rioja and Marlborough would score high).

cards.title: Operation Mincemeat
cards.question: Name anyone with an acting credit in the 2021 film Operation Mincemeat, or possibly the stage musical cast (Colin Firth would score high).

picks.note: Olivier winner for the musical - if the stage show counts

cards.title: The 2024 Summer Paralympics
cards.question: Name any sport on the Paris 2024 Paralympic programme, or any ParalympicsGB gold medallist from those Games (Sarah Storey would score high).

picks.note: multiple swimming golds in Paris, low profile

cards.title: 'Race Across The World'
cards.question: Name any country contestants have travelled through in a UK series of Race Across the World, or possibly any winning pair (route lists need verifying against each series).

picks.note: series 1 leg en route to Singapore
series 1 winners - winners' names are near-pointless

cards.title: Cinematic Brians
cards.question: Name any feature film with 'Brian' in its title, or a notable film character called Brian (Life of Brian would score high).

picks.note: 1971 American TV-movie classic, little known in the UK
John Cleese's headmaster, if characters count

cards.title: Fantasy Authors
cards.question: Name any author from a published list of great or best-selling fantasy authors (Tolkien, Rowling and Pratchett would score high).

picks.note: huge sales, low name recognition with a general 100
Gormenghast - literary enough to be forgotten as 'fantasy'

cards.title: Films By 'Wolf Hall' Actors
cards.question: Name any feature film with an acting credit for Mark Rylance or Damian Lewis (Wolf Hall's Cromwell and the Duke of Suffolk - Dunkirk or Bridge of Spies would score high).

picks.note: recent but instantly forgotten Rylance golf comedy

cards.title: Greek Cinema
cards.question: Name any feature film directed by Yorgos Lanthimos, or possibly any notable Greek film (The Favourite and Poor Things would score high).

picks.note: between Dogtooth and The Lobster, missed by most
Oscar-nominated but niche with a general 100
1960 classic if the question reaches further back

cards.title: Nu Metal Bands
cards.question: Name any nu metal band, likely from a published list (Linkin Park, Limp Bizkit and Korn would score high).

picks.note: scene staples, never crossed over in the UK

cards.title: 'American Pie'
cards.question: Name anyone with an acting credit in the 1999 film American Pie, or any word of four or more letters in the lyrics of Don McLean's American Pie.

picks.note: 'the courtroom was adjourned' - deep-cut lyric word
'no verdict was returned' - same verse, rarely recalled
played Kevin; the least-named of the four leads
the Sherminator - recognised face, unknown name

cards.title: 'Doctor Who' In The Ncuti Gatwa Era
cards.question: Name the title of any Doctor Who episode from Ncuti Gatwa's run (2023-2025), or any credited guest actor.

picks.note: acclaimed but hard to dredge up by title
the satire episode - titles score low across the board
played Ruby's mum Carla - guest names are near-pointless

cards.title: 'Wicked'
cards.question: Name any named character in Wicked, any song from the musical, or anyone with an acting credit in the 2024 film (Elphaba, Glinda and Defying Gravity would score high).

picks.note: Elphaba's sister, hard to dredge up
Wizard's number, deep in the track list

cards.title: 1980S Snooker
cards.question: Name any player who appeared in a World Snooker Championship final during the 1980s, or any winner of the UK Championship in that decade.

picks.note: 1981 world finalist and 1988 UK champion, rarely recalled today
won the 1986 world title at 150/1 and is routinely forgotten
1988 world finalist; fame faded faster than Davis or Higgins
1989 finalist - his title came in 1991, so the 80s final slips minds

cards.title: 40 Years Of 'Eastenders'
cards.question: Name any character, or the actor who played them, from the first episode of EastEnders in February 1985.

picks.note: shopkeeper written out within the first year
punk single mum from the original cast

cards.title: Animals On London Transport
cards.question: Name any London Underground, Overground or DLR station whose name contains an animal (Oxford Circus and Cockfosters would score; could also be the 'Animals on the Underground' map shapes).

picks.note: DLR station most people never think of
the raven hides in plain sight
hound - three stations for the price of one
Victoria line, often missed despite the horse mosaic

cards.title: Bob Marley On Stage And Screen
cards.question: Name anyone with an acting credit in the 2024 film Bob Marley: One Love, or in the musical Get Up, Stand Up! The Bob Marley Story.

picks.note: Olivier-nominated as Marley in the West End musical
played Don Taylor in One Love
Rita Marley in the film - Lashana Lynch would score higher
small One Love role under a famous surname

cards.title: Butterflies
cards.question: Name any butterfly species regularly found in the UK (Red Admiral and Peacock would score high).

picks.note: confined to a stretch of Dorset coast
lives in elm canopies, seen by almost no one

cards.title: Children's Book Illustrators
cards.question: Name any winner of the Kate Greenaway Medal (now the Carnegie Medal for Illustration) - Quentin Blake and Shirley Hughes would score high.

picks.note: the first medal winner, for Tim All Alone
illustrated Narnia and Tolkien's maps; 1968 winner
two-time winner, unknown outside the trade
Meg and Mog artist, won for Haunted House
We're Going on a Bear Hunt - book famous, name not

cards.title: Christmas Desserts
cards.question: Name any traditional Christmas dessert, probably from a published international list (Christmas pudding and yule log would score high).

picks.note: Danish rice pudding with a hidden almond
Spanish nougat - known to travellers, not to the 100
saying it in French rather than 'yule log' may drop the score

cards.title: European Football Title Winners From North London
cards.question: Name any player who appeared in a European final won by Arsenal or Tottenham (Spurs: 1963 Cup Winners' Cup, 1972 and 1984 UEFA Cups; Arsenal: 1970 Fairs Cup, 1994 Cup Winners' Cup).

picks.note: scored twice in the 1963 final - almost nobody under 70 knows it
played in Arsenal's 1994 win over Parma

cards.title: French Royalty
cards.question: Name any King of France, with regnal number where needed (Louis XIV and Louis XVI would score high).

picks.note: 'the Posthumous' - reigned five days as a newborn in 1316
the Quarrelsome; gone within two years
the Tall, lost between famous Philips
died hitting his head on a door lintel

cards.title: Irish Politics
cards.question: Name anyone who has served as Taoiseach of Ireland since 1937 (Leo Varadkar and Bertie Ahern would score high).

picks.note: two terms, declared the Republic, almost never recalled
the moderniser - big in Ireland, low with a UK panel

cards.title: Ragtime Music
cards.question: Name any Scott Joplin composition, or any noted ragtime composer (The Entertainer and Maple Leaf Rag would score high).

picks.note: late Joplin, loved by pianists only
Joplin deep cut with a familiar tune
the white New Yorker of the big three composers
the forgotten third of the ragtime trinity

cards.title: Singing Carlys
cards.question: Name any UK top 40 single by an artist called Carly - most plausibly Carly Simon or Carly Rae Jepsen (You're So Vain and Call Me Maybe would score high).

picks.note: Simon's 1982 Chic-produced hit - big then, hazy now
1987, remembered as a song more than a title

cards.title: West Indies Cricket
cards.question: Name any member of the West Indies squads that won the 1975 or 1979 World Cups, or possibly any country or territory the West Indies team represents (Viv Richards and Clive Lloyd would score high).

picks.note: the Dutch half counts too, and nobody knows it
